methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Oxford Quantum Circuits is a Series B quantum computing company that has raised $100M+ to date. The quantum computing sector contains 11 identifiable comparable companies: 3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers including Q-CTRL, Quantinuum, and others.

Stage-progression analysis applies here, using the company's $100M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-subsequent-stage progression. This method yields $190M. A peer-median approach could not be reliably applied; while 7 quantum computing peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables produced insufficient datapoints (fewer than 3 matches required for statistical confidence). Relying on all-sector medians would introduce excessive noise.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $162M to $218M, with a median point of $190M. This range reflects the output of a single applicable method, which constrains confidence. Key uncertainties include the absence of a robust peer-set median cross-check, the use of total raised as a valuation proxy (which may not reflect current market conditions or technical progress), and limited visibility into the company's revenue, unit economics, or IP differentiation relative to the public comparables in the sector.
